questions: Which company earning more than $1B total across all quarters has the worst satisfaction rating?
Looking at the jump from first-quarter to fourth-quarter revenue, which company saw the biggest gain?
Which private company has the largest headcount?
Among privately held firms, which is the only one that experienced negative growth in exactly one quarter?
Which publicly traded company IPO'd most recently?
Which company produced the largest total revenue across the entire year (all four quarters added together)?
Which company with debt-to-equity under 2.0 and satisfaction over 6.4 posted the largest fourth-quarter revenue?
Averaging the growth rates for Q1 through Q4, which company comes out on top?
Which company has the second-earliest founding year overall?
Which company with a decline in Q3 carries the highest D/E ratio?
constraints: Must include the headquarters city of the company that answers Question 1.
Must include the CEO's last name of the company that answers Question 5.
Must include the headquarters country of the company that answers Question 6.
Must include a prime number equal to nextPrime((employees of the answer to Question 1 mod 100) + 11), as digits.
Must include the equation "A+B=C" (digits, no spaces) where A=((Q1 revenue of the answer to Question 1 mod 90)+10) and B=((Q4 revenue of the answer to Question 6 mod 90)+10) and C=A+B.
Acrostic: the first letters of the first 8 words must spell the first 8 letters of (initials(Q1)+initials(Q5)+initials(Q6)+initials(Q4)), uppercased.
Tip: avoid extra punctuation-only "words"; words are split on spaces.
doc:
# THE EVENING LEDGER — BUSINESS

Xeno Industries reports a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.9 and a satisfaction score of 7.4 out of 10.
